latest.title: Farm-In to JHIâ€™s North Falklands Basin Licence
latest.content:
2 Mar 2026 17:05
RNS Number : 0021V
Westmount Energy Limited
02 March 2026
2 March 2026
WESTMOUNT ENERGY LIMITED
("Westmount" or the "Company")
Navitas executes Farm-In to JHI's North Falklands Basin Licence, PL001
Westmount Energy Limited (
UK AIM:
WTE.L), the AIM-quoted oil and gas investing company focussed on high impact drilling outcomes in emerging basins, notes the announcements today by Navitas Petroleum LP (
"Navitas"
)
1
and Westmount investees JHI Associates Inc. (
"JHI"
)
2
and Eco (Atlantic) Oil & Gas Ltd. (
"Eco"
)
3
pertaining to the execution of a farm-in agreement by Navitas and JHI under which Navitas will acquire a 65% Working Interest (
"W.I."
) and operatorship of PL001 North Falklands Basin Licence (
"PL001"
). JHI will retain a 35% W.I. which continues to be held via its wholly owned subsidiary JHI Falkland Inc. (
"JHIF"
). As consideration, in addition to payment of its share of JHIF's past costs in connection with the Licence, Navitas
will fund JHI's 35% W.I. share of the costs associated with potential future activities, including an exploration well and potential appraisal well, up to an aggregate cap of USD$14M. This funding will be provided via a loan-carry arrangement repayable from 85% of JHIF's free cash flow from production under the PL001 License.
PL001 is located immediately to the west of PL032 containing the Navitas operated Sea Lion Project which recently achieved Final Investment Decision with respect to Phase 1 of this giant development complex with over 700 MMbbls of recoverable resources.
PL001, which covers approximately 1,126 square kilometres, is located in modest water depths (<500m), is fully covered by 3D seismic and contains significant exploration potential. JHI's internal Best Estimates indicate a prospect inventory containing an aggregate 3.1bn bbls of prospective recoverable resources with an aggregate upside of more than 10 bn bbls
2
. So far, two oil prospects, Tyche and Dinlas, have been high-graded within the same stratigraphic interval as the Sea Lion accumulation - each containing a potential 400 MMbbls recoverable
2
.
Westmount holds 1,500,000 shares in Eco. In addition, Westmount holds a total of 5,685,257 shares in JHI, representing approximately 6.24% of the issued share capital.
1
https://maya.tase.co.il/he/reports/companies/1725338
2
https://www.jhiassociates.com/north-falkland-basin
3
https://polaris.brighterir.com/public/eco_atlantic_oil_gas_plc/news/regulatory_news/story/xe478nw
For further information, please contact:
Westmount Energy Limited
www.westmountenergy.com
David King, Director Tel: +44 (0) 1534 823000
Cavendish Capital Markets Limited
(Nomad and Broker) Tel: +44 (0) 20 7397 8900
Neil McDonald / Pete Lynch

latest.title: Navitas Farm-In to JHI’s North Falklands Licence
latest.content:
13 Jan 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 5858O
Westmount Energy Limited
13 January 2026
13th January 2026
WESTMOUNT ENERGY LIMITED
("Westmount" or the "Company")
Navitas to Farm-In to JHI's North Falklands Basin Licence, PL001
Westmount Energy Limited (
UK AIM:
WTE.L), the AIM-quoted oil and gas investing company focussed on high impact drilling outcomes in emerging basins, is pleased to report that its investee JHI Associates Inc.
("JHI"
) has signed a non-binding Memorandum of Understanding (
"MOU"
)
1
with Navitas Petroleum LP (
"Navitas"
) for a farm-in under which Navitas will acquire a 65% Working Interest (
"W.I."
) and operatorship of the PL001 North Falklands Basin Licence (
"PL001"
). JHI currently holds 100% W.I. in PL001 via a wholly owned subsidiary, JHI Falkland Inc. (
"JHIF"
). PL001 is located immediately to the west of PL032 containing the Navitas operated Sea Lion Project which recently achieved Final Investment Decision with respect to Phase 1 of this giant development complex with over 700 MMbbls of recoverable resources.
PL001, which covers approximately 1,126 square kilometres, is located in modest water depths (<500m), is fully covered by 3D seismic and contains significant exploration potential. JHI's internal Best Estimates indicate a prospect inventory containing an aggregate 3.1bn bbls of prospective recoverable resources with an aggregate upside of more than 10 bn bbls
2
. So far, two oil prospects, Tyche and Dinlas, have been high-graded within the same stratigraphic interval as the Sea Lion accumulation - each containing a potential 400 MMbbls recoverable
2
.
JHI is a private, Ontario-registered, company established in 2014 and focused on oil exploration opportunities in the emerging Guyana-Suriname and North Falklands basins. Westmount holds a total of 5,685,257 shares in JHI, representing approximately 6.24% of the issued share capital.
Gerard Walsh Chairman of Westmount Energy Ltd. commented:
"We are delighted to see this significant follow-on transaction on the back of the recent reawakening of interest in our offshore Falkland Islands and Guyanese assets.
A tie-up of the North Falklands Basin PL001 Licence with the incumbent operator of the giant Sea Lion Development presents significant potential opportunity in terms of synergies re sub-surface understanding, rig sharing and production facilities in the success case."
1
https://maya.tase.co.il/he/reports/1716562
2
https://www.jhiassociates.com/north-falkland-basin
For further information, please contact:
Westmount Energy Limited
www.westmountenergy.com
David King, Director Tel: +44 (0) 1534 823000
Cavendish Capital Markets Limited
(Nomad and Broker) Tel: +44 (0) 20 7397 8900
Neil McDonald / Pete Lynch
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. company_30s.what_they_do: WTE Energy is a niche exploration firm that focuses on offshore oil and gas assets in Guyana, mainly the North Falklands Basin. It holds minority stakes in larger operators JHI and ECO and is pursuing a farm‑in to develop new licences.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Guyana’s offshore blocks have become one of the most talked‑about growth areas for global oil supply. By partnering with established players, WTE can tap proven infrastructure, lower development risk, and potentially secure a share of the region’s sizeable reserve upside.
company_30s.current_state: Still in the exploration stage with no commercial output. The recent farm‑in with JHI signals a strategic push into a high‑potential basin, but the company remains cash‑burning and trades at a steep discount to comparable peers.

rns_narrative: WTE has secured a farm‑in with JHI for the North Falklands Basin, giving it access to a promising offshore licence.
Corporate updates show a steady stream of AGM results, holdings disclosures, and a new corporate presentation, indicating a focus on transparency and investor communication.
Cross‑shareholdings with JHI and ECO suggest a coordinated strategy to leverage joint assets and share risk.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism waiting for the farm‑in to unlock value
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls highlight the farm‑in deal and the company’s ties to larger operators, viewing it as a catalyst for production and valuation lift.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry about the company’s tiny size, the uncertainty of Guyana’s regulatory environment, and the risk that the farm‑in may not translate into commercial output.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The community was largely indifferent until the farm‑in announcement, after which chatter turned mildly bullish, but overall engagement remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for WTE is flat—no public search interest, which means a surprise catalyst could generate a big reaction.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the farm‑in with JHI and a formal drilling schedule for the North Falklands Licence.
Positive drilling results or discovery of commercial‑grade reserves that can be announced in the next RNS.
Any regulatory approval or partnership announcement that moves the company closer to first‑oil.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike and a breakout above the 200‑day moving average; a press release that confirms drilling start or a new reserve estimate would also confirm the thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock sits in the early stages of a deep crash‑recovery cycle. Capitulation appears complete, but mean reversion will only kick in once a tangible catalyst—like drilling results—materialises.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The farm‑in gives WTE access to a high‑potential block with proven operators. If the company delivers a commercial discovery, the price could climb to the mid‑range of its historical lows, yielding a 200‑plus percent upside while the downside is capped at the current price level.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company remains in the exploration phase, with no proven reserves yet. Regulatory delays, cost overruns, or a lack of commercial output could keep the stock stuck at its current low, making a full recovery unlikely.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential gain: 2–3× current price if production is announced; potential loss: limited to the current price because the stock is already heavily discounted.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a clear volume surge and a price break above the 200‑day SMA, with a stop 10% below entry. Size the position to 5–10% of your discretionary capital and monitor drilling updates closely.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until a drilling schedule or reserve announcement is released. Watch for a 30‑day moving average breakout as a secondary confirmation.
playbook.if_passing: No immediate terminal risk identified; the setup remains attractive until a delisting or bankruptcy event occurs.

ai_insights.bottom_line: WTE sits at a deep crash level but the new farm‑in with JHI offers a clear catalyst for mean reversion, making it a contrarian bullish play with asymmetric upside and capped downside.
